published.text:
### Hero
Heading: AI Automation for Pest Control Companies
Body: Opens with urgency scenario — homeowner finds termites, calls three companies, first to respond gets the job. Frames automation around recurring service renewals, seasonal pest campaigns, and techs-on-route missed calls.
Sub: Below: what pest control automation looks like in practice, where it delivers the clearest ROI, and how to decide if it is worth the investment.

### Core sections
- What you can automate in a pest control company (cards: missed call text-back, lead follow-up, recurring service reminders, seasonal campaigns, quote follow-up, review requests)
- Is this a good fit? (evaluation: techs on route, seasonal business, 2+ technicians vs solo operator fully booked from referrals)
- ROI expectations (considerations: recurring client value $500-$2K/yr, urgency-driven leads, seasonal spikes, recurring revenue protection)
- Results from similar service businesses (case studies with pest control framing)

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers for pest control companies considering automation
5 questions covering most impactful automation, recurring service management, seasonal campaigns, cost, software compatibility

### Final CTA
Heading: Stop losing pest control jobs to missed calls and lapsed renewals

edits.description: Published as part of batch 5. Targets pest control companies with recurring revenue models and seasonal lead spikes.
Interlinks to landscaping companies, cleaning companies, instant lead response, client reactivation.
Recurring revenue angle is the strongest ROI argument for this vertical.
